intent.oneSentenceIntent: A macOS app that shows near-real-time Russian subtitles for the English you hear — on YouTube and in video calls — by translating your Mac's system audio with a high-quality realtime speech-translation engine.
targetUsers: a Russian-speaking Mac user who watches English YouTube videos and joins video calls with English speakers, and wants to follow them in Russian in real time
intent.problem: following spoken English in real time is hard; existing options are either low-quality, not real-time, or cannot tap the audio you actually hear (a YouTube video, the other people in a call)
intent.desiredOutcome: start a session and read high-quality Russian subtitles, with about 1-3 seconds of lag, for whatever English audio is playing on the Mac
usageContexts: in video calls and conferences with English speakers
explicitConstraints: translation QUALITY is the top priority
near-real-time: subtitles with about 1-3 seconds of lag are acceptable; this is not zero-latency simultaneous interpretation
English to Russian, one direction only
uses cloud providers because quality is the priority
bring-your-own-key: the user can enter their own provider key in the app
nonGoals: Russian to English (translating the user's own speech)
fully offline operation (a private on-device mode is a possible future path, not this build)
saving or exporting transcripts (may be added later)
translating language pairs other than English to Russian
capturing the user's microphone or own voice
embedding or shipping API keys inside the app

coreJourneys.actor: a Russian-speaking user watching an English YouTube video
coreJourneys.trigger: the user starts a translation session and plays the video
preconditions: a provider API key has been entered in Settings
Screen Recording permission has been granted (first run)
steps: read the Russian subtitles in the floating window
coreJourneys.expectedOutcome: Russian subtitles track the spoken English in near-real-time (about 1-3s lag), faithful to the meaning
coreJourneys.failureRecovery: if the network or audio stream drops, the window shows a clear 'reconnecting' state and resumes without crashing; if no key is set, it guides the user to add one; if there is no speech, it shows a calm idle state

coreJourneys.actor: a Russian-speaking user in a video call with English speakers
coreJourneys.trigger: the user starts a translation session at the start of the call
preconditions: a provider API key has been entered in Settings
Screen Recording permission has been granted
steps: start a translation session before or during the call
the remote participants speak English (their audio plays through the Mac's speakers = system audio)
the app captures that system audio, transcribes and translates it
the user reads the Russian subtitles in the floating window
coreJourneys.expectedOutcome: the user follows what the English speakers are saying in Russian, near-real-time; the user's own microphone and voice are never captured
coreJourneys.failureRecovery: the same reconnecting and idle handling as JRN-001; the user can stop the session at any time and the subtitles clear

wont: language pairs other than EN to RU

sources.title: Maxim's request: high-quality real-time EN->RU translation of system audio for YouTube and video calls; cloud engine; ScreenCaptureKit capture; bring-your-own-key with in-app key entry plus system trial keys for experiments
ScreenCaptureKit captures system audio natively (SCStreamConfiguration.capturesAudio), macOS 13+, 48kHz/stereo — no third-party audio driver required

live captioning/translation tools (e.g., Apple Live Captions, Otter) establish the streaming subtitle-overlay pattern; their recurring weaknesses are latency and accuracy on fast or overlapping speech
DeepL produces high-quality translations, with its strongest reputation on European language pairs; it supports Russian among 33 languages
2026 streaming speech-to-text comparison: Deepgram Nova-3 leads streaming latency (sub-300ms, ~6.84% median streaming WER); AssemblyAI Universal-3 Pro and ElevenLabs Scribe v2 are close competitors
2026 LLM-vs-MT translation benchmark: frontier LLMs (GPT-4/GPT-4o, Claude) lead COMET and human evaluation for nuanced, idiomatic translation; DeepL's edge is European pairs

2026 unified realtime speech-translation: OpenAI GPT-Realtime-Translate and Google Gemini 3.5 Live Translate stream audio in and translated transcript+audio out in a single model over WebSockets, sub-second to a few seconds latency, positioned as interpreters

Apple SpeechAnalyzer / SpeechTranscriber (WWDC25, macOS 26): on-device speech-to-text built for live transcription, ~55% faster than Whisper Large V3 Turbo at matching accuracy — the strongest on-device ASR for a future private path

analogClaims.claim: real-time captioning tools transcribe streaming speech and display it as an overlay; their recurring weaknesses are latency and accuracy on fast or overlapping speech
DeepL produces high-quality machine translation, strongest on European language pairs; Russian is supported but not its standout strength
ScreenCaptureKit natively captures system audio without third-party drivers on macOS 13+

in 2026 the leading approach to live speech translation is a unified realtime model (OpenAI GPT-Realtime-Translate, Google Gemini 3.5 Live Translate) that streams audio in and translated transcript out in a single hop with sub-second to few-second latency
for nuanced English-to-Russian, frontier LLMs (GPT-4o, Claude) are competitive with or better than dedicated MT engines on quality
Apple's on-device SpeechAnalyzer/SpeechTranscriber (2025) is ~55% faster than Whisper Large V3 Turbo at matching accuracy — the strongest on-device ASR for a future no-key private path

baseline.statement: capture the Mac's system audio output (the English speech from YouTube and from call participants) as the input to translate
convert the English speech to text in real time via a streaming engine (a unified realtime speech-translation model, or a streaming speech-to-text provider)
display the Russian translation as near-real-time subtitles in a floating, always-readable window over any app
stay alive through network or audio-stream interruptions: show a clear reconnecting state and resume without crashing
bring-your-own-key: let the user enter and securely store their own provider API key in the Keychain; the app ships with no embedded keys

customDelta.statement: translate the English to high-quality Russian using a high-quality engine; the meaning is faithfully conveyed
start and stop a translation session; stopping clears the subtitles
a clear first-run / no-key state that guides the user to add a key before a session can run
readable subtitle presentation (size, position, opacity) that stays legible over varied backgrounds and in light/dark
bound latency to a near-real-time target (about 3 seconds) by translating on sentence boundaries (pipeline) or using a streaming realtime model (unified)

qualityAttributes: subtitles readable over any background, light or dark
resilient to network drops — never crashes
private by configuration — zero-retention providers, keys in the Keychain, no embedded secrets
captures only system audio, never the microphone
provider-agnostic — the engine sits behind a protocol so it can track the fast-moving 2026 market

dataFlows.dataCategory: system audio (English speech from YouTube or call participants)
dataFlows.purpose: stream to a cloud realtime speech-translation provider (unified model), or to a streaming speech-to-text provider in the alternative pipeline, to obtain Russian text
dataFlows.retention: none — configure the provider for zero retention; the app does not store it
dataFlows.deletion: not persisted; exists only for the live session

dataFlows.purpose: translate to Russian when using the ASR+MT pipeline rather than a unified model
dataFlows.retention: none — configure the provider to not retain / opt out of training; the app does not store it
dataFlows.deletion: not persisted; exists only for the live session

dataFlows.dataCategory: provider API keys (the user's own; or owner/system trial keys for experiments later)
dataFlows.source: user input in Settings (or Doppler for the owner's local development)
dataFlows.purpose: authenticate to the chosen cloud providers
dataFlows.retention: stored until the user removes it
dataFlows.deletion: the user can clear it in Settings

forbiddenPractices: never embed or hardcode API keys in the app bundle or source code
store keys only in the Keychain — never in UserDefaults or a plist
never log API keys, raw audio, or transcripts
do not persist audio or transcripts beyond the live session unless the user explicitly opts in
never capture the microphone or the user's own voice

capabilityNeeds.minimalScope: system audio only — not screen pixels, not the microphone
capabilityNeeds.purpose: obtain the English speech to translate
capabilityNeeds.userBenefit: translate the English the user hears on YouTube and in calls

capabilityNeeds.capability: network access to cloud speech and translation providers
capabilityNeeds.minimalScope: outbound HTTPS/WebSocket streaming to the chosen realtime translation or speech-to-text + translation endpoints only
capabilityNeeds.userBenefit: the translation quality the user prioritized
use their own provider account securely without re-entering keys

compatibilityConstraints: requires a provider API key (bring-your-own-key) to run live

successCriteria: an English YouTube video produces readable Russian subtitles in near-real-time
in a video call, the remote speakers' English appears as Russian subtitles; the user's own voice is never captured
the translation is faithful enough to follow the meaning (verified on a real sample clip)
a dropped network or audio stream shows a clear reconnecting state and recovers without crashing
the app runs on a provider key the user entered in Settings, and ships with no embedded keys

acceptanceTests.given: English audio is playing through the Mac's system output and a session is started
then: audio buffers reach the translation pipeline (non-zero frames)

acceptanceTests.given: a known English utterance enters the pipeline (a fake engine emits a final Russian transcript for it)
then: the corresponding Russian text is produced via the engine protocol
it is rendered as a subtitle line within the latency budget

acceptanceTests.given: a real English sample clip and a real engine (a unified realtime model, or Deepgram + an LLM/DeepL) using the owner's keys
acceptanceTests.when: the clip is played through the app
then: the Russian subtitles are accurate enough to follow the meaning (reviewed by the user)

acceptanceTests.when: the user starts a session and later stops it
then: starting begins capture and subtitles appear on speech
stopping ends capture and clears the subtitles

acceptanceTests.when: the engine or network connection drops and then returns
then: the app shows a clear reconnecting state
it resumes and renders subsequent subtitles without crashing

acceptanceTests.given: the user enters a provider API key in Settings
acceptanceTests.when: the key is saved and later read back
then: it is persisted in the Keychain and used for requests
when no key is present the app shows the add-key guidance and makes no cloud call

unknowns.description: configure the chosen providers for zero data retention / opt out of training
unknowns.safeDefault: enable each provider's no-retention/opt-out setting and document it

unknowns.description: real-world end-to-end latency under a live network
unknowns.safeDefault: prefer a unified realtime model for lowest latency; in the pipeline variant translate on sentence-final boundaries; measure and tune in the live smoke

unknowns.description: ongoing per-minute/character cost under heavy daily use
unknowns.safeDefault: document the hourly cost; keep the engine swappable, including a future on-device path (Apple SpeechAnalyzer + Apple Translation) for a no-key private mode

unknowns.description: providing owner/system trial keys to non-owner users for experiments
unknowns.safeDefault: do NOT embed keys in the client; offer trial keys only via a quota-limited server proxy in a later slice; until then, bring-your-own-key only
unknowns.discoveryTrigger: deciding to let other people experiment with system keys

unknowns.description: the audience may broaden from personal to public/other users
unknowns.safeDefault: treat as personal/direct now; broadening triggers the proxy and a hardened privacy review
unknowns.discoveryTrigger: deciding to distribute beyond personal use

unknowns.description: which concrete provider is primary for the live smoke (OpenAI GPT-Realtime-Translate vs Gemini 3.5 Live Translate vs Deepgram+LLM)
unknowns.safeDefault: abstract behind the engine protocol; default the live smoke to whichever provider the owner has a key for; OpenAI Realtime is the working default

unresolvedRisks: private video-call audio is transmitted to a third-party cloud provider — accepted under the explicit quality-first choice, mitigated by zero-retention configuration and capturing only system audio
'high quality' is subjective and cannot be machine-asserted — it is verified via a live smoke on sample clips, reviewed by the user
near-real-time latency depends on the network and the provider; sub-second is not guaranteed
the 2026 provider market is moving fast (Gemini 3.5 Live Translate shipped weeks ago); the engine protocol must keep providers swappable
offering system trial keys to other users is unsafe without a server proxy; embedding keys would expose them to extraction

decisionLog.rationale: the user's stated priority is translation QUALITY; cloud is easier to guarantee at a high bar than on-device today

decisionLog.decision: ScreenCaptureKit for system-audio capture (it triggers the Screen Recording permission)
decisionLog.rationale: native, with no third-party audio driver to install; the user chose it

decisionLog.decision: abstract the 'English audio -> Russian text stream' seam behind a provider protocol; do not hardwire one vendor
decisionLog.rationale: 2026 research shows the field shifting fast (unified realtime speech-translation models shipped weeks ago); abstracting the seam lets the app use the best current provider and swap as the market moves

decisionLog.decision: primary engine: a unified realtime speech-translation model (OpenAI GPT-Realtime-Translate or Google Gemini 3.5 Live Translate) streaming English audio in and Russian transcript out in one hop
decisionLog.rationale: 2026 research: both ship interpreter-grade single-model streaming with sub-second to few-second latency and transcript deltas; one hop beats a two-stage pipeline on latency and simplicity for live interpretation

decisionLog.decision: documented alternative pipeline: Deepgram Nova-3 streaming speech-to-text -> an LLM translator (GPT-4o/Claude) or DeepL
decisionLog.rationale: Deepgram Nova-3 leads streaming latency/WER; LLMs lead nuanced EN->RU in COMET/human eval (DeepL's edge is European pairs); kept swappable for control and cost

decisionLog.decision: future private/offline path: Apple SpeechAnalyzer on-device speech-to-text + Apple Translation on-device EN->RU
decisionLog.rationale: Apple's 2025 on-device SpeechTranscriber is ~55% faster than Whisper Large V3 Turbo at matching accuracy; enables a no-key private mode later behind the same protocol

decisionLog.decision: one-way EN->RU; capture system audio, never the microphone
decisionLog.rationale: the user wants to understand English speakers; their audio is already in the system output; the user's own voice is out of scope

decisionLog.decision: bring-your-own-key — keys entered in Settings, stored in the Keychain; no embedded keys
decisionLog.rationale: the user asked for in-app key entry; embedding keys in a client app would expose them to extraction and account drain

decisionLog.decision: the owner's keys are used only for local development and the live smoke via Doppler — never committed or bundled
decisionLog.rationale: safe testing at real quality without exposing the keys

decisionLog.decision: system trial keys for other users deferred to a proxy-backed later slice
decisionLog.rationale: the only safe way to share keys is server-side behind a quota-limited proxy

decisionLog.decision: quality verified by a live smoke; pipeline correctness verified by automated tests on fakes
decisionLog.rationale: subjective quality cannot be unit-asserted; the pipeline can

buildMayDecide: the concrete primary provider among the researched options and its SDK/transport (WebSocket)
buildMustPreserve: the engine sits behind a swappable provider protocol
capture system audio only — never the microphone
never crash on a stream/network drop — reconnect
bring-your-own-key with Keychain storage and NO embedded keys
stopConditions: any requirement needs the user's own microphone or voice
any requirement persists call audio or transcripts without explicit consent
any plan embeds API keys in the bundle or source
translation quality falls below the live-smoke bar
verificationEvidenceRequired: automated pipeline assertions on fakes (text flow, subtitle render, reconnect, latency budget)
a live smoke against a real engine on a sample English clip with the owner's keys, with quality reviewed by the user

changeLog: rev1: initial intent contract — awaiting human confirmation of the recap

rev2: baked in 2026 stack research (S5-S8, C4-C6); engine decision = provider protocol with a unified realtime model primary (OpenAI/Gemini), Deepgram+LLM/DeepL alternative, Apple on-device future path; corrected the DeepL-is-RU-king assumption; status -> ready
